rulings.Case Title: FISCAL FUNDING CO. INC. VS. ALLEN MATKIS LEEK GAMBRE MALLORY LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees Incurred On Appeal And In Enforcing Judgment
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 10, RESPONDENTS ALAN DONES AND JOHN GUILLORY'S Motion For Attorney'S Fees Incurred On Appeal And In Enforcing Judgment. Respondents Alan Dones and John Guillory's motion for attorney's fees incurred on appeal and in enforcing judgment is continued to August 23, 2018 to give moving parties an opportunity to file billing records and plaintiffs Calvin Grigsby and Fiscal Funding Co., Inc. an opportunity to file opposition papers based on their review of those billing records. No later than August 10, 2018, Mr. Doherty must file a further supplemental declaration attaching the billing records reflecting the work he performed for which his clients are seeking fees on this motion. No later than August 17, 2018 plaintiffs may file supplemental opposition papers based on their review of those billing records. While there is case law stating that billing records are not always necessary to decide a fees motion, there is also case law stating that a court may require that the billing records be provided. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. KINK.COM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT CYBERNET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Cybernet Entertainment, LLC's demurrer to the eleventh cause of action for violation of the Unruh Act is overruled. The statute of limitations does not bar plaintiff's Unruh Act claim as it relates back to the filing of the original complaint. The Unruh Act claim is based on the same general set of facts as plaintiff's other claims. The original, first amended, and second amended complaints all include allegations regarding the disparate treatment between homosexual and heterosexual actors. (Complaint, par. 28; First Amended Complaint, par. 31; Second Amended Complaint, par. 54.) The claims involve the same injury. The discriminatory conduct allegedly led to plaintiff's contraction of HIV. Cybernet's argument that the Unruh Act claim fails because that Act does not apply to workplace discrimination claims lacks merit. Per Payne v. Anaheim Memorial Medical Center (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 729, the Unruh Act can apply to an independent contractor such as plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: VIMLABEN PATEL VS. NARENDRA SHARMA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Void Default Judgment Exceeding The Amount Demanded In Complaint
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT NARENDRA SHARMA'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Void Default Judgment Exceeding The Amount Demanded In Complaint. Defendant Narenda Sharma's motion to set aside the judgment filed February 8, 2018 is granted unless plaintiff Vimlaben Patel agrees to entry of an amended judgment superseding the February 8 judgment in the amount of $21,325. Even if the February 8 judgment is not void, allowing it to remain in effect violates Mr. Patel's due process rights and would be a miscarriage of justice. The principal and prejudgment interest amounts in the February 8 judgment cannot be reconciled with Ms. Patel's verified complaint and motion for judgment on the pleadings. Moreover, the large increase in attorney's fees from the filing of the motion for judgment on the pleadings to the uncontested trial is not supported by anything in the record. Per the verified complaint and motion for judgment on the pleadings, Ms. Patel is at most entitled to a judgment of $21,325, comprised of principal of $12,500, prejudgment interest of $6,250, and attorney's fees of $2,575. Arguably no fees should be included in the amended judgment since the CCP and CRC provide that the setting of contractual fees should be done on a post-judgment motion, not at trial. The court has assumed that an additional $1000 in fees was incurred from the filing of the motion for judgment on the pleadings to the uncontested trial. If Ms. Patel agrees to the amended judgment by submitting a proposed amended judgment in conformity with this tentative ruling at or before the hearing, this motion will be denied. Otherwise, the February 8 judgment will be set aside and a new trial date will be set. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Unless Ms. Patel agrees to the amended judgment, Mr. Sharma is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: TERRY BUTTS VS. FARMERS INSURANCE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANTS TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE AND MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendants Truck Insurance Exchange and Mid-Century Insurance Company's motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendants fail to show that Mr. Butts cannot establish his bad faith claim. While the genuine dispute doctrine might apply to any dispute over the applicable legal test to determine coverage, it does not apply to the parties' dispute about the provable facts bearing on whether Christian Somerville had the implied permission of his parents to drive the Suburban. Since there are both legal and factual disputes in this case, MCIC and Truck cannot avail themselves of the genuine dispute doctrine. (See Mt. Hawley Insurance Company. v. Lopez (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 1385, 1424, n. 20.) Moreover, there is a triable dispute whether the insurers' conducted a reasonable investigation into the possibly provable facts, especially after Mr. Scherrer's letter of June 6, 2013 stating that "we have identified coverage issues that will require additional investigation" and their receipt of the arbitration award in the underlying case. There are also triable disputes whether the insurers had fraudulent intent regarding their communications with Mr. Somerville and whether they acted with malice or oppression, as those terms are defined in Civil Code 3294(c), by clear and convincing evidence. Based on the possibly provable facts known to the insurers by the time they received the arbitration award, and likely much before then, there are triable issues whether their denial of a duty to defend was known to them to be fraudulent and constituted despicable conduct in willful disregard of the rights of Mr. Somerville and Mr. Butts and causing cruel and unjust hardship to one or both of them. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Butts is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: TERRY BUTTS VS. FARMERS INSURANCE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Truck Insurance Exchange And Mid-Century Insurance Company'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Added For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Purposes Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 4, PART 2 OF 2 TENTATIVE RULING :Defendants Truck Insurance Exchange And Mid-Century Insurance Company'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Added For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Purposes Only) . The fact that the letter was written with regard to the Vance and Allen claims and prior to the tender of the Mr. Butts' complaint in the underlying action is immaterial since the same coverage dispute was involved in all three claims. The insurers' heavy reliance on Government Employees Insurance Company v. Kinyon (1981) 119 Cal. App. 3d 213 and Mucci v. Winter (1951) 103 Cal. App. 2d 627 is misplaced. Neither case involved an issue of implied permission by a parent to a child driver, and Mucci was expressly distinguished on that point by Elkinton. While the policy language in Kinyon is similar to the policy language in the MCIC policy, the insurers ignore that the subjective belief of the driver test enunciated in that case is not mutually exclusive of the "inferable state of mind of the alleged permitter" test that applies to determining implied permission. That latter test, which did not apply to Kinyon because that was a non-owned vehicle case, is applicable to this case due to Insurance Code 11580.1(b)(4), which statutorily mandates coverage for any driver who had the implied permission of the named insured with respect to an owned vehicle of the named insured. Based on that test, even if not the subjective belief test, there is a triable dispute whether the possibly provable facts known to MCIC and Truck by the time they received the arbitration award created a possibility of coverage, thereby triggering a duty to defend Mr. Somerville. Farmers Insurance and Farmers Insurance Group's request for summary judgment in their favor is granted. Mr. Giles' declaration which showed that "Farmers Insurance" and "Farmers Insurance Group" are merely trademark names and they are not subject to being sued establishes that there is no dispute that those trademark names cannot be held liable for any of the claims asserted by Mr. Butts in this case. Mr. Butts' objections to Mr. Giles' declaration are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Butts is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=302/HK

rulings.Case Title: TERRY BUTTS VS. FARMERS INSURANCE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 4, PART 1 OF 2 TENTATIVE RULING, DEFENDANTS TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE AND MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendants Truck Insurance Exchange and Mid-Century Insurance Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Per Elkinton v. California State Automobile. Association, Interstate Insurance Bureau (1959) 173 Cal.App.2d 338, 344, which remains good law, only slight evidence is necessary to find implied permission to operate a vehicle where a parent-child relationship is involved. (See also Pierce v. Standow (1958) 163 Cal.App.2d 286 (finding implied permission where the mother gave the keys to her son even though the mother expressly told the son not to drive the car).) No later than their receipt of the judicial arbitration award in the underlying case, and likely much before then, defendants knew of the existence of possibly provable facts that viewed in the light most favorable to coverage showed that at the very least there was a possibility of coverage under the policies issued by MCIC and Truck. In the words of the arbitrator, those facts included that "the parents [of Christian Somerville] knew that¿[Mr. Somerville] had a propensity to try and take whatever it was he wanted," Mr. Somerville "had taken and passed a driving course ¿ and had expressed interest to his parents in obtaining his permit/driver's license," and "[e]ven when his parents had discovered that¿[Mr. Somerville] had taken items from their bedroom, they¿did not lock their bedroom door or dresser drawer where¿[Mr. Somerville]¿obtained the 'spare keys' to the Suburban." As many cases have held, '[i]f coverage depends on an unresolved dispute over a factual question, the very existence of that dispute would establish a possibility of coverage and thus a duty to defend.'" (North Counties Engineering, Inc. v. State Farm General Ins. Co. (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 902, 922.) Moreover, Mr. Scherrer's letter of June 6, 2013 stating that "we have identified coverage issues that will require additional investigation" is a concession that there is a possibility of coverage which itself triggered a duty to defend.

rulings.Case Title: ARKADY BERGER VS. LOSCH & EHRLICH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Motion To Compel Further Responses And Compliance (Per Ost 7/31/18)
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 11, Defendants' Motion To Compel Further Responses And Compliance (Per Ost 7/31/18). Defendants' motion to compel further responses and compliance is granted in small part. Plaintiff Arkday Berger need not provide any further responses to the discovery seeking information about his payment of bills sent to him by any attorneys other than defendants because that information is not relevant nor likely to lead to the discovery of any admissible evidence. Information about Mr. Berger's payment or non-payment of other attorneys' bills does negate or tend to negate any element of any of Mr. Berger's claims or any of defendants' affirmative defenses. Mr. Berger need not provide any further responses regarding his communications with any attorneys regarding those attorneys holding funds collected in the Varum matter because those communications are within the attorney-client privilege and the privilege has not been waived as to those communications. Unless he disclaims in writing that he is seeking any damages for emotional distress, Mr. Berger must provide a verified code-compliant further response to document request 25 no later than August 13, 2018. Mr. Berger must also provide a verified code-compliant further response to special interrogatory 11 no later than August 13, 2018. Mr. Berger is not required to provide any further responses to any other discovery request. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Berger is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDRIY KRAVCHUK VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Of Special Reference Appointing Judge David A. Garcia (Ret.) Special Master For The Purpose Of Hearing Any And All Discovery Motions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ANDRIY KRAVCHUK's Motion For Order Of Special Reference Appointing Judge David A. Garcia (Ret.) Special Master For The Purpose Of Hearing Any And All Discovery Motions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for appointment of a Discovery Referee is denied. The sole reason proffered for the appointment is his attorney fees will arguably be lessened if a Discovery Referee rules on an excessive discovery request dispute. The record reflects no attempt whatsoever to meet and confer on excessive discovery requests were served. The appropriate response to service of excessive discovery is resolved either through the meet and confer process or by a timely filed motion for a protective order. rulings.Case Title: CHARTER ASSET MANAGMENT FUND, L.P. VS. LATIN GRAMMAR SCHOOL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine Prevailing Party And Fix Attorneys Fees As Costs
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT LATIN GRAMMAR SCHOOL, INC. AND LATIN COLLEGE PREPARATORY CHARTER SCHOOL, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine Prevailing Party And Fix Attorneys Fees As Costs. Defendants Latin Grammar School, Inc. and Latin College Preparatory Charter School, Inc.'s motion to for attorneys' fees is denied. Defendants' successful motion to quash was not a determination on the merits of the case, but solely a ruling on whether they are subject to being sued in California on the claims of plaintiff Charter Asset Management Fund, L.P. The findings made on the motion to quash were made for the sole purpose of determining whether this court could exercise specific personal jurisdiction over defendants and were not intended to and presumably are not binding on any merits issue, although that issue may need to be litigated in Georgia. Because the merits of the contract claims have not been determined and CAM has stated that it intends to pursue those claims in Georgia, defendants are not prevailing parties for purposes of a fees award. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for CAM is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JANE AM DOE VS. KARIM ZAIM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Third Party San Francisco District Attorneys Office To Produce Privilege Log And Responsive Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT KARIM ZAIM's Motion To Compel Third Party San Francisco District Attorneys Office To Produce Privilege Log And Responsive Documents. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is continued to September 13, 2018 by service of an Amended Notice of Motion filed July 19, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ARYA KHOSRAVI VS. GRAY LINE CORPORATION INC. DBA GRAY LINE OF SAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of New Hearing Date Re Defendant Blue Bus Tours Company Llcs Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT BLUE BUS TOURS COMPANY LLC's Motion To Compel. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Parties to appear. The Court intends to hear argument and rule on adequacy of the following the supplemental discovery responses: Form Interrogatories General, Set One, No. 8.7; Form Interrogatories Employment, Set One, No.'s 201.1. 207.1. 210.2 and 213.1; Special Interrogatories, Set One, No.'s 6, 31-36, 38, 40, 42, 45-47, Requests for Production, Set One, No.'s 21, 22. Also to be ruled upon is whether a privilege log is required and if sanctions should be imposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO INDEPENDENT TAXI ASSOCIATION VS. LOUIS NMEZIE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Louis Nmezie
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 7, PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO INDEPENDENT TAXI ASSOCIATION's Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Louis Nmezie. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's unopposed motion deeming admitted facts and genuineness of documents is unopposed and granted. Sanctions in the sum of $1,000.00 are awarded to plaintiff, which shall be paid within twenty days after service of a copy of the Order herein with Notice of Entry. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION VS. SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to all causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff San Francisco Credit Union is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DEXTER DUARTE VS. AUGMEDIX INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Notice Of Appeal Of Order, Decision, Or Award Of The Labor Commissioner
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2018, Line 9, RESPONDENT AUGMEDIX INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Notice Of Appeal Of Order, Decision, Or Award Of The Labor Commissioner. Defendant and appellee Augmedix Inc.'s motion to dismiss appeal of a decision of the Labor Commissioner is granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)
